name: sil
description: Turn your OpenClaw into an expert shopping agent.

sil_web_url.description: Override for the sil-web origin (the auth authority — token refresh, registration). Falls back to the SIL_WEB_URL env var, then https://sil.4gpts.com.

sil_api_url.description: Override for the sil-api origin (the domain service — sil_whoami's identity read). Distinct from sil_web_url (sil-web). Falls back to the SIL_API_URL env var, then https://sil-api.4gpts.com. Set this at deploy to pin the sil-api host (or to the same origin as sil_web_url if a single gateway fronts both).

sil_web_url.label: sil-web URL (auth)
sil_web_url.help: The sil-web origin — handles registration and token refresh. Override only for staging or self-hosted deployments.

sil_api_url.label: sil-api URL (identity)
sil_api_url.help: The sil-api origin — sil_whoami reads the user's identity here. Distinct from the sil-web URL. Override for staging or self-hosted deployments.

security.packagingNote: Compiled JavaScript ships under ./dist. register() stays synchronous and opens nothing — it only registers tools. ALL I/O happens inside a tool's execute(): sil_register calls sil-web over the network, writes tokens.json + config.json to the plugin data directory (owner-only, 0600), and arms a bounded background poll timer that stops on the first terminal claim outcome or after the session deadline. sil_whoami reads the stored session token and calls sil-api for the user's identity, refreshing transparently via sil-web on a 401 (at most one refresh + one retry per call, no timer); a confirmed-dead session clears tokens.json. sil_search and sil_product_get read the stored session token and call sil-api's catalog endpoints (search / lookup) with it — read-only on catalog data, no timer, but with the SAME bounded 401 recovery as sil_whoami: on a 401 they refresh ONCE via sil-web (rotating tokens.json) and retry the read ONCE; a second 401 or a dead (invalid_grant) refresh token clears tokens.json and is reported as a terminal re-register hint. A non-401 error writes no token. The five profile verbs make NO network call and read no token; they write the one sil shopper's SINGLETON behaviour artefacts under a fixed $SIL_DATA_DIR/shopper/ (owner-only, files 0600, dirs 0700) as frontmatter-as-truth markdown — there is NO profile.json manifest; each file's own frontmatter IS the source of truth, discovered by a filesystem scan. sil_profile_materialize is SETUP-ONLY: it writes shopper/user_spec.md (the shared person spec; its frontmatter carries the shopper name), minting no domain. sil_learn is the single target+change verb over shopper/domains/<slug>/{method.md, prds/<product>-<intent>.md, assets/}: create mints a whole method or PRD, fail-closed if one already exists; write replaces an existing doc's whole body with a caller-authored reconciled version, fail-closed if the target is absent — there is no in-place section edit, so every change rewrites the whole document; attach-asset persists image bytes (size-capped, image MIME allowlist) into the domain's assets/ owner-only and links them by RELATIVE path, never inline bytes. sil_profile_search scans artefact FRONTMATTER and returns coordinates only (no bodies) — a domain/PRD with malformed frontmatter is skipped and surfaced as unreadable, never silently dropped or half-read. sil_profile_get reads ONE whole body (method or PRD); sil_profile_remove deletes a whole validated domain subtree, or one PRD file, fail-closed on a malformed/traversal/"main" or missing slug (never the whole shopper). sil_doctor is a report-first diagnostic over the SAME $SIL_DATA_DIR state: it reads the data dir, file modes, tokens.json/config.json metadata, and artefact frontmatter, and returns a machine-readable findings array. It reads no token contents out and rotates no token: token health is metadata only (present / mode / parses / expired), where expiry is decoded from the access token's own JWT exp claim strictly LOCALLY (decode-only, no signature verification, no network, no claim other than exp extracted, never a token byte emitted or logged). Its only writes are two safe idempotent fixes under $SIL_DATA_DIR — tightening a too-open mode and creating the missing data dir; a tighten only ever NARROWS (it detects bits set outside the expected mask and chmods to mode & expected, which can only clear bits, never grant them). Enumerated entries under the data dir are lstat'd, and a symlink found among them is REPORTED, never chmod'd-through — that is what keeps a tighten inside the store. The data directory ROOT itself is stat'd rather than lstat'd, because chmod follows a symlink regardless (Linux exposes no lchmod), so lstat there would prevent nothing and merely re-report the same fix forever; a symlinked $SIL_DATA_DIR is an operator-declared indirection, so the tighten lands on the directory the plugin already stores everything in. anything that would mutate the bytes of an existing artefact (clearing a corrupt tokens.json, deleting an orphaned tmp file, repairing an artefact) is REPORTED as needs_confirmation and never run. It makes exactly ONE outbound request, and it is the only reason https://clawhub.ai is declared: an unauthenticated read-only GET of the published version of @4gpts/sil, bounded by an explicit timeout + abort, carrying no token, no PII, no store contents, and no Authorization header, pinned to that one host (redirects are refused). It fails soft to silence — an unreachable, slow, non-200, or unparseable response yields no finding at all and never fails the run. sil_doctor never acts on the version it reports: it installs nothing, spawns no process, writes no host config, and prompts nobody. The only caller-supplied path segments are the domain slug and the PRD key, both validated (lower-kebab, not "main") before any join; the shopper segment is a compile-time constant. Every write is atomic (tmp file → rename over target → chmod), so a reader sees a whole file or none. The persona is the shopper's host workspace SOUL.md, written by the host CLI (not this plugin, not a sil artefact), and the shopper's host-config wiring is driven separately by the host CLI. The PKCE verifier is held only in memory and is never written to disk. The plugin registers ONE gateway RPC method, sil.search_results (scope operator.read, which the host authorizes before the handler runs): a paired client calls it over the already-authenticated deviceToken WS to read back a sil_search page whose callId it already saw on the tool frames. It opens no socket, no port, and no route — registering it hands the host a closure, and the host owns the transport. The pages it serves live in a bounded IN-MEMORY buffer that is never written to disk: sil_search stores its own successful result there under the callId the host passed it (at most 32 pages, dropped 15 minutes after the search, evicted lazily on the next write with no timer, and gone entirely when the process exits). The method makes no network call, starts no agent run, and returns a page only to the sil account that produced it — every unknown, expired, or foreign reference gets one identical not-found body. Artefact bodies, tokens, and identity PII are never logged; the method logs only whether a page was found and how many products it held.
filesystemScope: $SIL_DATA_DIR (default $XDG_DATA_HOME/sil, else ~/.local/share/sil) — tokens.json, config.json, shopper/user_spec.md + shopper/domains/<slug>/{method.md, prds/<product>-<intent>.md, assets/<content-hash>.<ext>} (the ONE sil shopper's singleton behaviour artefacts, frontmatter-as-truth with NO profile.json manifest: sil_profile_materialize writes the shared user_spec.md at setup (its frontmatter carries the shopper name), and sil_learn mints/refines per-domain methods, intent-keyed PRDs, and per-domain image assets. The `shopper` segment is a fixed compile-time constant, not caller input; the domain slug and PRD key are the only caller-supplied path segments, validated (lower-kebab, not "main") before any join. All writes are atomic + owner-only (files 0600, dirs 0700). The persona is the host workspace SOUL.md, not a sil artefact)
